methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $200M - $3.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

data.narrative:
LanzaTech is a public-stage climate and energy company founded by Sean Simpson and Richard Forster. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 12 same-sector companies operating at similar valuation tiers, spanning 1 public company and 8 private firms including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The peer-median method, the primary triangulation approach applicable to LanzaTech's profile, yields an implied valuation of $850M based on the median across the 12-company comp set. This peer universe ranges from $200M at the low end to $3.5B at the high end, with sample peers including Form Energy ($3.5B, Series G), Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B, late stage), KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), and Watershed ($1.8B, Series G).

The triangulated valuation range is $722M to $977M, with a median of $850M. This falls marginally below LanzaTech's stated current valuation of $800M+, suggesting alignment at the lower bound of the stated range. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the peer set—valuations span 17.5x from floor to ceiling—and the limited number of applicable methodologies given single-method triangulation. Revenue multiples, capital efficiency, and technology maturity differentiation among peers remain important drivers of the observed dispersion.
